response: to arrange three indistinguishable rooks on a six by six board without any two attacking each other we must first choose three distinct rows out of the six available which can be done in twenty ways and then choose three distinct columns out of the six available which is also twenty ways for each pair of chosen rows and columns there are six ways to assign the rooks to the intersections since the rooks are indistinguishable we divide by six to account for the permutations of the rooks themselves resulting in a total of eight hundred distinct arrangements
